Container.Name: OpenScrub-Nvidia
Container.Overview: Local video redaction with human review — NVIDIA GPU build. Detects and blurs PII in videos and screen recordings — faces, license plates, names, SSNs, and more, plus custom regex categories — with GPU-accelerated OCR (PaddleOCR-CUDA), GPU face detection and identity grouping (CUDA OpenCV), GPU license-plate detection (onnxruntime CUDA), and NVENC hardware encoding. Works on all NVIDIA GPUs, Maxwell and newer. Requires the Nvidia Driver plugin. Everything runs on this server; no footage leaves your network. Best-effort redaction: always review output before sharing.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: redaction blur privacy PII PHI face license plate anonymize deface GPU CUDA NVENC
Container.Requires: Unraid Nvidia Driver plugin (Apps > Nvidia Driver) and an NVIDIA GPU (Maxwell or newer). First pull is large (~6.8 GB): the image ships CUDA, a CUDA-built OpenCV, and GPU OCR.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: HTTPS web interface (self-signed certificate — your browser warns once, or install your own cert in App settings).

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Jobs (contains the sensitive uploads and reports!), review decisions, certificates, zones, downloaded models, encryption vault. Everything OpenScrub stores lives here — the container itself is disposable.

Config.Name: Access token (optional)
Config.Description: Leave empty for open access on your LAN, or set a secret to require it on every request (recommended if others use your network). The WebUI URL becomes https://ip:8384/?token=SECRET on first visit.

Config.Name: Media
Config.Description: Optional read-only mount of your media share so the 'path on the server' picker can process files without uploading them through the browser.

Config.Name: OPENSCRUB_MEDIA_ROOT
Config.Description: Confines the server-path picker to this directory tree. Leave set to /media to match the Media mount; clear it to allow any container path.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
Config.Description: Which GPUs the container may use ('all', or a specific GPU UUID from the Nvidia Driver plugin page).

Config.Name: NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES
Config.Description: Leave as-is: 'video' is required for NVENC hardware encoding.

Config.Name: OPENSCRUB_CPU_DNN
Config.Description: Set to 1 to force face and plate detection back onto the CPU (troubleshooting).
